new_classes.definition_en: Whisky distilled in Scotland and matured there, marketed at an alcoholic strength of 40% vol or more, classified under heading 2208.

new_classes.label_en: Scotch whisky with added soda water in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2208 30 61)
new_classes.label_de: Scotch-Whisky mit Sodawasser in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2208 30 61)
new_classes.definition_en: Scotch whisky to which soda water has been added, presented in containers of a nominal capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under subheading 2208 30 61.

new_classes.label_en: Soda water added quality (whisky to which soda water has been added)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a whisky that has had soda water added to it, as described in CN subheadings 2208 30 61 and 2208 30 69.
A distillation process in which a grain mash is distilled to produce whisky, an alcoholic beverage marketed at 40% vol or more.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) whisky is distilled from grain mash (WhiskyGrainDistillationProcess); (2) marketed at ≥ 40% vol (decimalRange restriction on ScotchWhisky2208); (3) Scotch whisky is distilled and matured in Scotland (ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality, already in TBox); (4) the specific product at 2208 30 61 is Scotch whisky with added soda water (SodaWaterAddedQuality); (5) containers ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer). A minor gap remains: the container size threshold for whisky is ≤ 2 L (same as SmallContainer), which is already defined in the TBox and reused here. The soda water additive is modelled as a quality rather than as a physical part, which is a slight simplification. The exact sibling subheading 2208 30 69 (Scotch whisky with soda water in containers > 2 L) is not modelled in this call but is consistent with the pattern used. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the grain-mash origin criterion for the broader whisky category (22083011–22083088) can only be partially expressed via a named process class rather than a compositional restriction on raw material.
